# JenniNexus Server Troubleshooting - November 4, 2025

## Issues Detected

After deploying and running the permissions script, the following errors appeared in browser console:

### 1. Missing Generated JSON Files (404 Errors)
```
GET https://jenninexus.com/resources/playlists/generated_tags.json 404 (Not Found)
GET https://jenninexus.com/resources/playlists/generated_content_tags.json 404 (Not Found)
```

**Cause**: These files are dynamically generated by `scripts/import-youtube-tags.js` and are not included in the deploy package by default.

### 2. YouTube API Failures (502 Bad Gateway)
```
GET https://jenninexus.com/resources/api/get-youtube.php?playlist_id=recent_gaming 502 (Bad Gateway)
GET https://jenninexus.com/resources/api/get-youtube.php?playlist_id=diy_tutorials 502 (Bad Gateway)
GET https://jenninexus.com/resources/api/get-youtube.php?playlist_id=devlogs 502 (Bad Gateway)
GET https://jenninexus.com/resources/api/get-youtube.php?playlist_id=fl_studio_tutorials 502 (Bad Gateway)
```

**Cause**: PHP-FPM is either not running, misconfigured, or the YouTube API endpoints are failing.

### 3. Mobile Nav Button Z-Index
**Status**: ✅ FIXED in `mobile-improvements.css` (z-index: 1200 added)

---

## Resolution Steps

### Step 1: Check PHP-FPM Status

SSH into the server and check if PHP-FPM is running:

```bash
ssh root@jennidrop

# Check PHP-FPM service status
systemctl status php8.3-fpm

# If not running, start it
systemctl start php8.3-fpm

# Check for errors in PHP-FPM log
tail -n 100 /var/log/php8.3-fpm.log

# Check Nginx error log for PHP-FPM connection issues
tail -n 100 /var/log/nginx/error.log
```

### Step 2: Verify PHP-FPM Socket/Port Configuration

Check if Nginx is correctly configured to communicate with PHP-FPM:

```bash
# Check Nginx site config
cat /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/jenninexus.conf | grep -A 5 "fastcgi_pass"

# Verify PHP-FPM socket exists (if using Unix socket)
ls -la /run/php/php8.3-fpm.sock

# Or check if PHP-FPM is listening on TCP port (if using TCP)
netstat -tlnp | grep 9000
```

**Expected Nginx fastcgi_pass configuration:**
```nginx
# Either Unix socket:
fastcgi_pass unix:/run/php/php8.3-fpm.sock;

# Or TCP (less common):
fastcgi_pass 127.0.0.1:9000;
```

### Step 3: Test PHP Processing

Create a simple test file to verify PHP is working:

```bash
echo "<?php phpinfo(); ?>" > /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/test-php.php
chown www-data:www-data /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/test-php.php
chmod 644 /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/test-php.php

# Test via curl
curl https://jenninexus.com/test-php.php

# Clean up after testing
rm /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/test-php.php
```

If you see HTML output with PHP version info, PHP-FPM is working.

### Step 4: Check YouTube API Endpoint Permissions

Verify the API endpoint exists and has correct permissions:

```bash
ls -la /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/api/

# Should show:
# -rw-r--r-- www-data www-data get-youtube.php
# -rw-r--r-- www-data www-data get-patreon-posts.php
# -rw-r--r-- www-data www-data get-patreon-tiers.php

# If permissions are wrong, fix them:
chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/api/
chmod 644 /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/api/*.php
```

### Step 5: Test YouTube API Endpoint Directly

```bash
# Test the endpoint with a known playlist ID
curl -v "https://jenninexus.com/resources/api/get-youtube.php?playlist_id=recent_gaming"

# Check for PHP errors in the response
# A 502 error means PHP-FPM couldn't process the request
```

### Step 6: Check PHP Error Logs

The YouTube API script may be encountering PHP errors:

```bash
# Check PHP-FPM error log
tail -n 200 /var/log/php8.3-fpm.log

# Check for script-specific errors
grep "get-youtube.php" /var/log/php8.3-fpm.log | tail -n 50

# Common issues to look for:
# - Missing PHP extensions (curl, json)
# - Memory limits
# - Execution timeout
# - File permission errors
```

### Step 7: Verify YouTube Secrets Configuration

The API may be failing due to missing YouTube API credentials:

```bash
# Check if secrets.json exists (DEPRECATED - should use YouTube Data API key)
ls -la /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/playlists/secrets.json

# Note: YouTube API now requires server-side credentials
# Check environment variables or config files for YOUTUBE_API_KEY
```

### Step 8: Generate Missing Tag Files

The generated tag files need to be created on the server. You have two options:

**Option A: Generate files locally and deploy them**

On your local machine:
```powershell
# Run the tag import script (if Node.js is available locally)
cd C:\Users\Owner\Projects\www\jenninexus
node scripts\import-youtube-tags.js

# Verify files were created
dir public_html\resources\playlists\generated_*.json

# Then rebuild and deploy
.\scripts\build-and-deploy.ps1
```

**Option B: Generate files on the server**

SSH into the server:
```bash
ssh root@jennidrop

# Check if Node.js is available
node --version

# If Node.js is not installed, install it:
curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_18.x | bash -
apt-get install -y nodejs

# Navigate to the scripts directory
cd /var/www/jenninexus/scripts

# Run the tag generation script
node import-youtube-tags.js

# Verify files were created
ls -la /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/playlists/generated_*.json

# Fix permissions
chown www-data:www-data /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/playlists/generated_*.json
chmod 644 /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/playlists/generated_*.json
```

### Step 9: Reload Services

After making any configuration changes:

```bash
systemctl reload nginx
systemctl reload php8.3-fpm

# Or restart if reload doesn't work:
systemctl restart php8.3-fpm
systemctl restart nginx
```

### Step 10: Verify Fixes

```bash
# Test homepage
curl -I https://jenninexus.com

# Test YouTube API endpoint
curl "https://jenninexus.com/resources/api/get-youtube.php?playlist_id=recent_gaming"

# Test tag files
curl https://jenninexus.com/resources/playlists/generated_tags.json

# Monitor live errors
tail -f /var/log/nginx/error.log
```

---

## Quick Diagnosis Commands

Run these commands to quickly identify the issue:

```bash
ssh root@jennidrop

# 1. Check all services
systemctl status nginx php8.3-fpm | grep -E "Active:|Main PID"

# 2. Check if files exist
ls -la /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/api/get-youtube.php
ls -la /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/playlists/generated_*.json

# 3. Check recent errors
tail -n 50 /var/log/php8.3-fpm.log | grep -i error
tail -n 50 /var/log/nginx/error.log | grep -i "502\|upstream"

# 4. Test PHP-FPM directly
SCRIPT_FILENAME=/var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/api/get-youtube.php \
  REQUEST_METHOD=GET \
  QUERY_STRING="playlist_id=test" \
  cgi-fcgi -bind -connect /run/php/php8.3-fpm.sock

# 5. Check permissions
find /var/www/jenninexus/public_html/resources/api -type f -ls
```

---

## Common Fixes

### Fix 1: Restart PHP-FPM
```bash
systemctl restart php8.3-fpm
systemctl status php8.3-fpm
```

### Fix 2: Fix Permissions
```bash
cd /var/www/jenninexus
./scripts/fix-permissions-jenninexus-remote.sh
```

### Fix 3: Check Nginx Configuration
```bash
nginx -t
# If errors, edit /etc/nginx/sites-available/jenninexus.conf
systemctl reload nginx
```

### Fix 4: Enable PHP Extensions
```bash
# Check installed PHP extensions
php -m | grep -E "curl|json|mbstring"

# If missing, install them
apt-get install php8.3-curl php8.3-json php8.3-mbstring
systemctl restart php8.3-fpm
```

---

## Mobile Nav Fix Status

✅ **Fixed locally** - The mobile navigation button z-index has been increased to 1200 in `mobile-improvements.css`.

To deploy the fix:
```powershell
# On local machine
cd C:\Users\Owner\Projects\www\jenninexus
.\scripts\build-and-deploy.ps1

# Then deploy to server using your preferred method
```
